assertions.description: INCORRECT: design_analysis.cross_domain_signals reports SCL and SDA as crossing +3.3V and +5V domains between U1 (Si5351A) and U2 (Arduino_Nano). However, U1's VCC is on +5V (confirmed via ic_pin_analysis: pin 6 Vin connects to +5V net), and U2 operates its I2C at 5V. The +3.3V appears in U2's power_rails because the Arduino Nano has a 3.3V power_out pin (pin '3.3V', type=power_out) — it generates +3.3V as an output, not as its I/O supply. The analyzer includes power_out pins when building IC domain membership, causing the false cross-domain detection. The connectivity_issues correctly reports +3.3V as a single_pin_net connected only to U2's output pin.
